campaign_goal: Validate the pg_enigma campaign harness on the fixed `frontier_rebase/pr1394/train_gpt_human.py` base by enforcing downstream-only control-paired comparison first, then testing same-trace checkpoint/export selection, while carrying recurrence handoff and shared-trunk late branching as deferred next mechanisms once the harness exposes the needed primitives.
name: Checkpoint And Export Selector Family
why_keep: It is the cleanest consequential family that can run now: same-trace artifact choice may beat the default final checkpoint/export pair on downstream `score_bpb`.
minimal_probe: From one training trace, evaluate a bounded late-checkpoint window against a fixed small export menu, with 2 controls carrying the published default checkpoint/export choice.
expected_signal: A non-default checkpoint/export pair wins on downstream `score_bpb` by more than control spread, and the winner is not recoverable from final-step or pre-quant ranking alone.
pack_rationale: Run immediately after control-delta calibration because it is executable now, lane-aligned, and tests a first-order searchable object without inventing new training machinery.

source_candidate.worldview: The right pr1394_enigma harness test is to keep the published SP8192/GPTQ-embeddings/depth-recurrence/MuonEq-R/SDClip stack frozen as the control base and search over bounded transition or selection programs around it. The consequential opportunities are regime changes and artifact choice, not scalar nudges.
source_candidate.name: Checkpoint And Export Selector Family
source_candidate.mechanism: Search selector programs over late checkpoints and export choices on an otherwise fixed training trajectory, because pr1394 already shows meaningful separation between pre-quant, post-quant, and sliding-window outcomes and may be choosing the wrong final artifact by default.
source_candidate.broken_invariant: The final/default checkpoint plus default export path is automatically the best artifact once the training run is fixed.
source_candidate.not_local_tuning_reason: This changes what artifact is allowed to win. The searchable unit is a selector over candidate checkpoints and export realizations, not a minor export threshold retune.
source_candidate.measurement_plan: If the family is real, the downstream winner will come from a non-default checkpoint/export pair, and the ranking will disagree with simple final-step or pre-quant ranking often enough to exceed control noise.
source_candidate.smallest_decisive_probe: Use the same trained trajectory for a same-checkpoint bakeoff over a small late-checkpoint window and a small fixed menu of catalog export realizations, with downstream quantized_sliding_window as admission metric.
source_candidate.expected_observable: If the family is real, the downstream winner will come from a non-default checkpoint/export pair, and the ranking will disagree with simple final-step or pre-quant ranking often enough to exceed control noise.
source_candidate.falsifier: If the selector almost always returns the final/default artifact, or ranking differences are within control spread, the artifact-selection family is not doing real work.
source_candidate.novelty_vs_history: This directly tests a lane the repo keeps underusing: artifact choice as a first-order search object rather than an afterthought behind training patches.
source_candidate.self_check_summary: High lane integrity because it is judged entirely on downstream artifact metrics; clearly not local tuning because the winner artifact itself is the object under search.

reasons: This is the cleanest artifact-selection family in the batch: the searchable object is explicit and first-order.
The same-trace checkpoint/export bakeoff gives a crisp falsifier with no lane mixing.
It stays close to currently plausible harness behavior because it only needs a bounded late checkpoint window and fixed export menu.
